ddls:
CREATE TABLE "postcodes" (
  "postcode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each postcode */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the postcode is located */,
  "postcode" INTEGER /* Postcode number */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population in the postcode */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the postcode in square kilometers */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population in the postcode */,
  "median_income" REAL /* Median household income in the postcode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("postcode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dwelling_types" (
  "dwelling_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dwelling type */,
  "dwelling_type" TEXT /* Type of dwelling (e.g., house, semi-detached, apartment) */,
  "average_price" REAL /* Average price of the dwelling type */,
  "average_size_sqm" REAL /* Average size of the dwelling type in square meters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dwelling_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "household_structures" (
  "household_structure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each household structure */,
  "household_structure" TEXT /* Type of household structure (e.g., couple with no children, one parent with children) */,
  "average_size" REAL /* Average number of people in this household structure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("household_structure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_data" (
  "demographic_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic data entry */,
  "postcode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the postcode */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was recorded */,
  "average_rent" REAL /* Average monthly rent for dwellings */,
  "employment_rate" REAL /* Percentage of employed individuals */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Average education level (e.g., High School, Bachelor's, etc.) */,
  "house" INTEGER /* Number of houses */,
  "semi_detached" INTEGER /* Number of semi-detached dwellings */,
  "apartment" INTEGER /* Number of apartments */,
  "other_dwelling" INTEGER /* Number of other types of dwellings */,
  "no_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with no bedrooms (studios, etc.) */,
  "one_bedroom" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with one bedroom */,
  "two_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with two bedrooms */,
  "three_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with three bedrooms */,
  "four_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with four bedrooms */,
  "five_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with five bedrooms */,
  "six_or_more_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Number of dwellings with six or more bedrooms */,
  "couple_no_children" INTEGER /* Number of couples with no children */,
  "couple_with_children" INTEGER /* Number of couples with children */,
  "one_parent_with_children" INTEGER /* Number of one parent households with children */,
  "other_family" INTEGER /* Number of other family households */,
  "sole_person_household" INTEGER /* Number of sole person households */,
  "group_household" INTEGER /* Number of group households */,
  "mental_health_conditions" INTEGER /* Number of people with mental health conditions */,
  "total_people" INTEGER /* Total number of people */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_data_postcode_id FOREIGN KEY ("postcode_id") REFERENCES postcodes ("postcode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, researcher, administrator) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "data_accessed" TEXT /* Description of the data accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
